titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Edward O'Farrell to Margaret O'Brien, 8 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter from Sir Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), assistant under-secretary for Ireland, to Margaret O'Brien.
The writer refers to Mrs. O'Brien's application for permission to move her deceased husband's remains from one grave to another within the Protestant church graveyard in Midleton and instructs her to furnish written consent from the church authorities and the District Sanitary Authority, in this case, Midleton Urban District Council.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/10140/3

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland
The Green, Midleton, County Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Any reply to the communication should
be addressed to:—
THE UNDER SECRETARY,
DUBLIN CASTLE.
and the following number quoted,
9575. CHIEF SECRETARY'S OFFICE,
DUBLIN CASTLE 8th June, 1916. Madam With reference to your letter of the 23rd ultimo,
applying for permission to remove the remains of your
husband from one grave to another in the burial ground
attached to the Protestant Church at Midleton, Co.Cork,
I am directed by the lords justice to request that you will
be so good at to furnish the consents in writing of the
authority in whom the burial ground is vested and of the
Sanitary Authority of the district to the removal of the
remains. I am,
Madam,
your obedient Servant. E. O'Farrell Mrs. Margaret. O'Brien
The Green,
Midleton,
CO. CORK.
